arts.ttl: Arsenal fear nightmare Bukayo Saka injury scenario as update given
arts.sum: Arsenal reportedly initially feared that Bukayo Saka’s hamstring had become detached from the bone following his injury against Crystal Palace - although, in reality, it is thought that the winger will not require surgery. Mikel Arteta says the 23-year-old will be out for “many weeks” and could not provide a precise recovery time.

arts.ttl: Barcelona's Olmo appeal REJECTED as agent initiates Man Utd and Arsenal talks
arts.sum: The Commercial Court of Barcelona has reportedly rejected the Blaugrana’s appeal to register Dani Olmo for the remainder of the 2024-25 season. However, they are close to seeing Olmo depart the club without receiving a transfer fee as LaLiga aims for Barcelona to fix their current financial woes.

arts.ttl: Arsenal enter race to sign Matheus Cunha in January
arts.sum: Arsenal are interested in a deal to sign Matheus Cunha from Wolves in the January transfer window. Mikel Arteta has tracked the progress of the forward for several seasons and interest has accelerated following injuries to Bukayo Saka and Raheem Sterling. arts.ttl: Arsenal can use Saliba agent to help seal signing – Gunners advantage due to relationship
arts.sum: Arsenal have included Sevilla’s Lucien Agoumé in their target list, according to Foot Mercato. Sevilla took the defensive midfielder on loan in the winter 2024 market and made his stay permanent in the summer. Agoumé has been a regular this season, making 18 appearances, and his performances haven’t gone unnoticed. arts.ttl: Arsenal 'interested in Matheus Cunha' after Wolves forward starred against Man United as Mikel Arteta aims to bolster attack following Bukayo Saka injury blow
arts.sum: Arsenal are 'interested in Matheus Cunha' as Mikel Arteta aims to bolster his squad following the news that talismanic winger Bukayo Saka is set to be sidelined for an extended period of time.

arts.ttl: Arsenal vs. Ipswich: Why Mikel Arteta should avoid Everton, Tottenham repeat in Premier League battle
arts.sum: Arsenal expert Charles Watts tells Sports Mole why the Gunners should not struggle to break down Ipswich Town in the manner that they did against Everton.

arts.ttl: Arsenal transfer gamble finally pays off as brutal Mikel Arteta decision delivers title boost
arts.sum: For much of this season, Arsenal looked like they could have done with Emile Smith Rowe being back at the Emirates Stadium. Chelsea looked set to move four points clear of Arsenal once again as Enzo Maresca saw his side leading 1-0 in the 81st minute .
